channel.title: Diabetes on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Diabetes on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Take Vitamin D to prevent prediabetes from progressing into diabetes – American Diabetic Association – 2024
item.description:
• Vitamin D efficacy in type 1 and type 2 diabetes

Vitamin D efficacy in type 1 and type 2 diabetes

Journal of Bone and Mineral Metabolism  April 2024 https://doi.org/10.1007/s00774-024-01509-3

Tetsuya Kawahara, Yosuke Okada & Yoshiya Tanaka

It is well known that vitamin D has a profound effect on calcium and bone metabolism, but its influence on other organs (extraskeletal effect) has been proposed. Consistently, vitamin D deficiency is associated with an increased incidence of various diseases, including type 1 and type 2 diabetes, as reported by many observational studies. However, there has been no consensus on whether vitamin D deficiency is a causative factor in the incidence of diabetes mellitus. There have been no randomized controlled trials (RCTs) aimed at preventing the onset of type 1 diabetes with vitamin D intake. In addition, the results of RCTs evaluating the preventive effect of vitamin D supplementation on type 2 diabetes development have been inconsistent. The recent observational studies, randomized controlled trials, and meta-analyses are confirming that vitamin D or active vitamin D administration is effective in preventing the incident of type 1 and type 2 diabetes.

item.title: Vitamin D-A New Therapeutic Target in the Management of Type 2 Diabetes Patients
item.description:
• Vitamin D-A New Therapeutic Target in the Management of Type 2 Diabetes Patients

Vitamin D-A New Therapeutic Target in the Management of Type 2 Diabetes Patients

J Clin Med . 2024 Feb 28;13(5):1390. doi: 10.3390/jcm13051390.

Oana Albai 1 2 3, Adina Braha 1 2, Bogdan Timar 1 2 3, Ioana Golu 4, Romulus Timar 1 2 3

Background: Vitamin D is a fat-soluble vitamin that prevents cardiovascular diseases and diabetes mellitus (DM). The present research aimed to study the impact of 25-hydroxyvitamin D (25(OH)D) level on the health status of patients with type 2 DM (T2DM) hospitalized in the “Pius Brînzeu” Emergency Clinical County University Hospital in Timisoara, Romania.

item.title: Risk of Type II Diabetes was cut if half if had a predicted 30 ng of vitamin D
item.description:
• Vitamin D and risk of developing type 2 diabetes in the SUN project: a prospective cohort study
      • Diabetic Epidemic chart

Vitamin D and risk of developing type 2 diabetes in the SUN project: a prospective cohort study

AJ Endocrinol Invest. 2024 Mar 8. doi: 10.1007/s40618-024-02324-3

Valer-Martinez 1 2, C Sayon-Orea 3 4 5 6, J A Martinez 7 8, F J Basterra-Gortari 1 9 10, M A Martinez-Gonzalez 1 11 9 12, M Bes-Rastrollo 1 11 9

item.title: Many T1 diabetics in Finland are self-treating with more than 1200 IU of Vitamin D daily
item.description:
• The use of dietary supplements, and the association between supplemental vitamin D and glycaemic control in adult individuals with type 1 diabetes
      • Note: Finns may have the highest Vitamin D levels on the planet, so do not need much more

The use of dietary supplements, and the association between supplemental vitamin D and glycaemic control in adult individuals with type 1 diabetes

Diabet Med . 2024 Feb 14:e15308. doi: 10.1111/dme.15308

Aila J Ahola 1 2 3, Valma Harjutsalo 1 2 3 4, Per-Henrik Groop 1 2 3 5; FinnDiane Study Group

item.title: Gestational Diabetes best fought by Vitamin D plus probiotics – RCT review
item.description:
• Advancements in Nutritional Strategies for Gestational Diabetes Management: A Systematic Review of Recent Evidence

Advancements in Nutritional Strategies for Gestational Diabetes Management: A Systematic Review of Recent Evidence

J Clin Med . 2023 Dec 20;13(1):37. doi: 10.3390/jcm13010037.

Juan Carlos Sánchez-García 1 2, Ines Saraceno López-Palop 2, Beatriz Piqueras-Sola 1 3, Jonathan Cortés-Martín 1 2, Elena Mellado-García 1 4, Inmaculada Muñóz Sánchez 5, Raquel Rodríguez-Blanque 1 2 6

Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) is defined as hyperglycaemia first detected at any time during pregnancy with values lower than those determined by the WHO for diabetes diagnosis in adults. This pathology, with a worldwide prevalence of 13.4%, causes significant maternal and foetal risks. The first line of treatment consists of maintaining normo-glycaemia through an adequate diet and lifestyle changes. The aim is to synthesize the scientific evidence updating the nutritional recommendations for the effective management of GDM. A systematic review of the scientific literature was conducted following the PRISMA guidelines.

item.title: Off Topic: Xylitol reduces cavities, preterm births, osteoporosis, RTI, obesity, diabetes, etc. - several studies
item.description:
• Xylitol reduced preterm births 40%, and cavities in their infants by 70% - Dec 2023
      • Oral and Systemic Effects of Xylitol Consumption - May 2019
      • Xylitol Chewing Gums reduced risk of dental caries by 3.7X - Dec 1995
      • Health benefits of xylitol - July 2020
      • Effects of xylitol chewing gum and candies on the accumulation of dental plaque: a systematic review Review - Oct 2021
      • Some of 100’s of Xylitol videos

Xylitol reduced preterm births 40%, and cavities in their infants by 70% - Dec 2023

Xylitol and the Gateway Microbiome

item.title: Men with 20 years of type 1 Diabetes have the same vitamin D levels as controls
item.description:
Do not recall seeing vitamin D levels becoming the same as controls after decades for ANY Vitamin D disease

      • Vitamin D status in longstanding type 1 diabetes and controls. Association with upper extremity impairments
      • 67+ VitaminDWiki Diabetes pages with T1 or TYPE 1 in title

Vitamin D status in longstanding type 1 diabetes and controls. Association with upper extremity impairments

Ups J Med Sci. 2023 Nov 22:128. doi: 10.48101/ujms.v128.9888

Hans J Arnqvist 1, Per Leanderson 2, Anna Spångeus 3 4

item.title: T2 Diabetes is also fought by Vitamin K2 – several studies
item.description:
• Effect of vitamin K2 on type 2 diabetes mellitus: A review – 2018
      • VitaminDWiki – Diabetes category associations:

Effect of vitamin K2 on type 2 diabetes mellitus: A review – 2018

Diabetes Research and Clinical Practice - Vol 136, Feb 2018, Pages 39-51 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.diabres.2017.11.020

Yan Li a, Jie peng Chen b, Lili Duan b, Shuzhuang Li a

Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) continue to be a major public health problem around the world that frequently presents with microvascular and macrovascular complications. Individuals with T2DM are not only suffering from significant emotional and physical misery, but also at increased risk of dying from severe complications. In recent years, evidence from prospective observational studies and clinical trials has shown T2DM risk reduction with vitamin K2 supplementation. We thus did an overview of currently available studies to assess the effect of vitamin K2 supplementation on insulin sensitivity, glycaemic control and reviewed the underlying mechanisms. We proposed that vitamin K2 improved insulin sensitivity through involvement of vitamin K-dependent-protein osteocalcin, anti-inflammatory properties, and lipid-lowering effects. Vitamin K2 had a better effect than vitamin K1 on T2DM. The interpretation of this review will increase comprehension of the development of a therapeutic strategy to prevent and treat T2DM.

item.title: Metabolic Syndromes fought by Vitamin D in 6 ways
item.description:
• Editorial: The role of vitamin D in reducing the risk of metabolic syndromes
      • Metabolic Syndrome studies are also in other VitaminDWiki categories

Editorial: The role of vitamin D in reducing the risk of metabolic syndromes

Front. Endocrinol., 02 Oct 2023 Sec. Translational Endocrinology Vol 14 - 2023 https://doi.org/10.3389/fendo.2023.1293262

Ziad H. Al-Oanzi1* Abdel-Naser Elzouki 2,3,4

1 Dept of Clinical Laboratories Sciences, College of Applied Medical Sciences, Jouf U., Sakaka, Saudi Arabia

2 Dept of Medicine, Hamad General Hospital, Hamad Medical Corporation, Doha, Qatar

item.title: UV reduces cardiovascular and metabolic problems– Vitamin D plus Nitric oxide
item.description:
• Impact of ultraviolet radiation on cardiovascular and metabolic disorders: The role of nitric oxide and vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki – Noontime sun and D category:
      • VitaminDWiki - Sun is better than UV, which is better than Vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki - Health benefit of Sunlight is more than Vitamin D in the blood - many studies

Impact of ultraviolet radiation on cardiovascular and metabolic disorders: The role of nitric oxide and vitamin D

Photodermatol Photoimmunol Photomed. 2023 Sep 20. doi: 10.1111/phpp.12914

item.title: Diabetes increases chance of death (low vitamin D) - many studies
item.description:
• Mortality and Cardio problems of T2 diabetes and prediabetes – meta-analysis Aug 2023

Mortality and Cardio problems of T2 diabetes and prediabetes – meta-analysis Aug 2023

Serum 25(OH)D Concentration, Vitamin D Supplementation, and Risk of Cardiovascular Disease and Mortality in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes or Prediabetes: a Systematic Review and Dose-Response Meta-Analysis

The American Journal of CLINICAL NUTRITION https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ajcnut.2023.07.012 PDF is behind a paywll

Ahmad Jayedi ’ , Mojtaba Daneshvar , Aliyu Tijani Jibril , John D. Sluyter , Mary Waterhouse , Briony Duarte Romero 4, Rachel E. Neale4, JoAnn E. Manson5, Sakineh Shab-Bidar 2’

item.title: Type 2 Diabetes treated by Vitamin D (often 50,000 IU weekly) – meta-analysis
item.description:
• Serum and supplemental vitamin D levels and insulin resistance in T2DM populations: a meta-analysis and systematic review
      • Vitamin D deficiency and its associated factors among patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus: a systematic review and meta-analysis - Oct 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - 50,000 IU Vitamin D weekly is often recommended 200+ studies

Serum and supplemental vitamin D levels and insulin resistance in T2DM populations: a meta-analysis and systematic review

Xingxing Lei, Qian Zhou, Yanmei Wang, Shunlian Fu, Zinan Li & Qiu Chen

item.title: T1 Diabetes increased by 27% by second year of COVID – meta-analysis
item.description:
• Incidence of Diabetes in Children and Adolescents During the COVID-19 Pandemic – A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
      • 65+ VitaminDWiki Diabetes pages with T1 or TYPE 1 in title
      • This increase in T1 may be due to COVID’s deactivation of Vitamin D Receptor
      • VitaminDWiki COVID infections and vaccinations decrease Vitamin D – many studies
      • See also web

Incidence of Diabetes in Children and Adolescents During the COVID-19 Pandemic – A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

JAMA Netw Open. 2023;6(6):e2321281. doi:10.1001/jamanetworkopen.2023.21281

item.title: FDA drug to TREAT child Type 2 diabetes (based on 1 trial of only 50)
item.description:
• FDA Approves 2 New Diabetes Drugs for Kids — Critic Says Clinical Trial Sample Was ‘Shockingly Low’

FDA Approves 2 New Diabetes Drugs for Kids — Critic Says Clinical Trial Sample Was ‘Shockingly Low’

The Defender

  • “The drugs — Jardiance (empagliflozin) and Synjardy (empagliflozin and metformin hydrochloride) — previously were approved for adults with Type 2 diabetes to improve blood sugar control.”

  • “But critics like pediatrician Dr. Elizabeth Mumper said the agency’s action ignores the root causes of Type 2 diabetes in children while generating profits for pharmaceutical companies :”

item.title: Diabetes 30% more likely in those with low birth weight
item.description:
• Birthweight is associated with clinical characteristics in people with recently diagnosed type 2 diabetes
      • increasing Vitamin D decreases low birth weight (and thus decreases early diabetes)
      • Vitamin D given to any child also decreases risk of diabetes

Birthweight is associated with clinical characteristics in people with recently diagnosed type 2 diabetes

Diabetologia. 2023 Jun 12. doi: 10.1007/s00125-023-05936-1

Aleksander L Hansen 1 2, Reimar W Thomsen 3, Charlotte Brøns 4, Helene M L Svane 3, Rasmus T Jensen 5, Mette K Andersen 5, Torben Hansen 5, Jens S Nielsen 6, Peter Vestergaard 7, Kurt Højlund 6, Niels Jessen 8, Michael H Olsen 9 10, Henrik T Sørensen 3, Allan A Vaag 11 12 13

item.title: Vitamin D both reduces CVD directly and reduces metabolic problems that cause CVD
item.description:
• The Role of Vitamin D in Reducing the Risk of Metabolic Disturbances That Cause Cardiovascular Diseases
      • Metabolic Syndrome Associations with 12 other categories
      • VutaminDWiki - Hypertension category contains
      • VitaminDWiki - The risk of 44+ diseases at least double with poor Vitamin D Receptor
      • VitaminDWiki - Vitamin D Receptor activation can be increased in 14 ways

The Role of Vitamin D in Reducing the Risk of Metabolic Disturbances That Cause Cardiovascular Diseases

J. Cardiovasc. Dev. Dis. 2023, 10(5), 209; https://doi.org/10.3390/jcdd10050209

item.title: 11 Diabetes problems are associated with low Vitamin D – review
item.description:
• The Impact of Vitamin D Deficiency and Insufficiency on the Outcome of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Patients: A Systematic Review

The Impact of Vitamin D Deficiency and Insufficiency on the Outcome of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Patients: A Systematic Review

Nutrients 2023, 15(10), 2310; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu15102310

Zaleha Md Isa ORCID,Norizzati Amsah ORCID and Norfazilah Ahmad *ORCID

Vitamin D deficiency and insufficiency are public health concerns that have contributed to multiple negative health outcomes. Recent studies have revealed that vitamin D deficiency and insufficiency influence glycaemic control and the development of diabetes complications.

item.title: 4X reduction in prediabetes progressing to T2D if more than 50 ng of vitamin D – RCT
item.description:
• Vitamin D and Risk for Type 2 Diabetes in People With Prediabetes : A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Individual Participant Data From 3 Randomized Clinical Trials

Vitamin D and Risk for Type 2 Diabetes in People With Prediabetes : A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Individual Participant Data From 3 Randomized Clinical Trials

Ann Intern Med. 2023 Mar;176(3):355-363. doi: 10.7326/M22-3018   PDF is behind a paywall

Anastassios G Pittas 1, Tetsuya Kawahara 2, Rolf Jorde 3, Bess Dawson-Hughes 4, Ellen M Vickery 1, Edith Angellotti 5, Jason Nelson 1, Thomas A Trikalinos 6, Ethan M Balk 6

item.title: T2 Diabetes might be fought by Vitamin D plus Vitamin D Receptor activators
item.description:
• Vitamin D in Diabetes: Uncovering the Sunshine Hormone’s Role in Glucose Metabolism and Beyond
      • Risk of 44+ diseases at least double with poor Vitamin D Receptor
      • 81+ Vitamin D Receptor pages in VitaminDWiki have CANCER in title
      • 14 ways Vitamin D Receptor activation can be increased
      • 6 Reasons that medical professionals do not prescribe Vitamin D for Diabetes

Vitamin D in Diabetes: Uncovering the Sunshine Hormone’s Role in Glucose Metabolism and Beyond

Nutrients 2023, 15, 1997. https: / / doi.org/ 10.3390/nu15081997

item.title: Diabetes 3X more likely if had COVID ICU (VDR was deactivated)
item.description:
• Severe COVID-19 linked to new diabetes diagnoses
      • VitaminDWiki - Vitamin D Receptor deactivated by some health problems - many studies
      • Taking Vitamin D and VDR activators would probably greatly reduce the risk of future Diabetes

Severe COVID-19 linked to new diabetes diagnoses

MDEdge

  • “COVID can more than triple the chance of being diagnosed with type 2 diabetes within a year of being infected, according to a new Canadian study.”

VitaminDWiki - Vitamin D Receptor deactivated by some health problems - many studies

Taking Vitamin D and VDR activators would probably greatly reduce the risk of future Diabetes


Markdown:
--------
{{< toc >}} 

---

#### Severe COVID-19 linked to new diabetes diagnoses

 **[MDEdge](https://www.mdedge.com/familymedicine/article/262540/covid-19-updates/severe-covid-19-linked-new-diabetes-diagnoses)** 

* "COVID can more than triple the chance of being diagnosed with type 2 diabetes within a year of being infected, according to a new Canadian study."

---

#### VitaminDWiki - [Vitamin D Receptor deactivated by some health problems - many studies](/posts/vitamin-d-receptor-deactivated-by-some-health-problems-many-studies)

---

#### Taking Vitamin D and VDR activators would probably greatly reduce the risk of future Diabetes

Original Tiki:
-------------
{DIV(class="lefth4")}{maketoc Title=""}{DIV} 
---
!!!!Severe COVID-19 linked to new diabetes diagnoses
__[https://www.mdedge.com/familymedicine/article/262540/covid-19-updates/severe-covid-19-linked-new-diabetes-diagnoses|MDEdge]__
*"COVID can more than triple the chance of being diagnosed with type 2 diabetes within a year of being infected, according to a new Canadian study."
---
!!!!VitaminDWiki -  {SQL(db=>vitamind)}SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT catObjectId, COUNT(*) FROM `tiki_category_objects` WHERE categId = 155  OR categId = 142 GROUP BY catObjectId HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) AS categs INNER JOIN tiki_objects ON tiki_objects.objectId = categs.catObjectId WHERE type = "wiki page"{SQL}       studies in both categories Virus and Vitamin D Receptor
''This list is automatically updated''
{category id="155+142" types="wiki" sort="created_desc" split="n" and="y" sub="n"- showdescription="n" showname="y" showtype="n" one="y" showTitle="n"}
---
!!!!VitaminDWiki -  {SQL(db=>vitamind)}SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT catObjectId, COUNT(*) FROM `tiki_category_objects` WHERE categId = 17  OR categId = 142 GROUP BY catObjectId HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) AS categs INNER JOIN tiki_objects ON tiki_objects.objectId = categs.catObjectId WHERE type = "wiki page"{SQL}       studies in both categories Diabetes and Vitamin D Receptor
''This list is automatically updated''
{category id="17+142" types="wiki" sort="created_desc" split="n" and="y" sub="n"- showdescription="n" showname="y" showtype="n" one="y" showTitle="n"}
---
!!!!VitaminDWiki - ((Vitamin D Receptor deactivated by some health problems - many studies))
---
!!!!Taking Vitamin D and VDR activators would probably greatly reduce the risk of future Diabetes 
---
!!!!! There have been ~~#00F: {SQL(db=>vitamind)}SELECT hits FROM tiki_pages WHERE page_id=14391{SQL}~~ visits to this page 
~tc~ (alias(Diabetes 3X more likely if had COVID ICU (VDR de-activation) - April 2023)) ~/tc~
~tc~ (alias(Diabetes 3X more likely if had COVID ICU (VDR was de-activated) - April 2023)) ~/tc~

item.title: Type 2 diabetes attributable to suboptimal diet in 184 countries (note: Vit D, Mg help)
item.description:
• Incident type 2 diabetes attributable to suboptimal diet in 184 countries
      • Note: 16 Diabetes and Mg studies as of April 2023
      • See also: Red meat consumption increases risk of Diabetes - many studies
      • Optimal dietary patterns for prevention of chronic disease - March 2023

Incident type 2 diabetes attributable to suboptimal diet in 184 countries

Nature Medicine volume 29, pages 982–995 (2023)

item.title: High fluoride in water: 5X more likely to be Vitamin D deficient
item.description:
• Vitamin D deficiency in patients with diabetes and its correlation with water fluoride levels
      • High Fluoride concentations in ground water in areas with Vitamin D deficiency
      • Fluoride in Volcanic Areas: A Case Study in Medical Geology- May 2019
      • Global analysis and prediction of fluoride in groundwater - 2022
      • Fluoride in natural waters - 2013
      • Excess fluoridation reduces Magnesium, and causes health problems

Vitamin D deficiency in patients with diabetes and its correlation with water fluoride levels

J Water Health . 2023 Jan;21(1):125-137. doi: 10.2166/wh.2022.254.

item.title: Diabetes, Metabolic Syndrome and Magnesium - many studies
item.description:
• Diabetes treated by Magnesium - Jan 2023
      • Ionized Magnesium Is Inversely Associated with Type 2 Diabetes - April 2022

Diabetes treated by Magnesium - Jan 2023

Serum Magnesium is Inversely Associated with Body Composition and Metabolic Syndrome

Diabetes, Metabolic Syndrome and Obesity Jan 2023 Vol 16 Pg 95—104 https://doi.org/10.2147/DMSO.S391369

Al Shammaa A, Al-Thani A, Al-Kaabi M, Al-Saeed K, Alanazi M, Shi Z zumin@qu.edu.qa

Human Nutrition Department, College of Health Sciences, QU Health, Qatar University, Doha, Qatar
